I press my palms against the cold hull of the dreadnought to hear the dying screams of the ship's machine-spirit as the warp-drive fractures.
I sift through the static of a thousand dead relays to channel the ancestral whispers of emperors who fell before the first star dimmed.
I cast the blackened bones of a void-whale across the navigation deck to divine which sectors of the rim have already been swallowed by the silence.
I brew a tea of bioluminescent fungi gathered from the ruins of a capital world to induce visions of the ley lines that once bound the quadrants together.
I guide the fragmented consciousness of the planetary governor back from the digital ether, stitching his shattered mind together with threads of psychic residue.
I cast a circle of salt around the boardroom table to keep the predatory spirits of the acquiring firm from poisoning the negotiations.
I have entered a deep trance to commune with the ancestral ghosts of the founding CEOs, seeking the hidden truth behind the discrepancies in the balance sheets.
I burn bundles of sage over the legal documents to cleanse the fine print of any deceptive curses or malicious intentions.
I read the flight patterns of urban crows over the financial district to determine the precise moment the shareholders will swing their vote.
I offer a sacrifice of expensive scotch and cigars to the gods of the market, praying they grant us a favorable closing price.
I have cast the bleached knuckle-bones upon the red earth to discern which clan the Great Spirit favors in this blood-feud.
My voice rattles like a dried gourd as I warn the chieftains that the forest will swallow us all if we do not cease this raiding.
I have brewed a tea of bitter roots and nightshade to dull the pain of the warriors wounded by the rival tribe's obsidian spears.
I danced until the world blurred into shadow, seeking a vision that would reveal the secret hiding place of the stolen livestock.
I feel the ancestors stirring in the wind, their restless ghosts demanding a sacrifice of gold-leaf and smoke to settle this ancestral grudge.
I plunge my consciousness into the city's optic nerve to purge the necrotic glitches haunting the residential sectors.
I negotiate with the temperamental spirit of the central power grid to ensure the neon arteries continue to pulse through the midnight smog.
I translate the binary screams of a crashing mainframe into ancestral warnings for the citizens who have forgotten how to listen.
I weave protective sigils from discarded fiber-optic cables to shield the slums from the predatory algorithms of the corporate spires.
I guide the fragmented ghosts of deleted personas back into the great data-stream so they may find peace in the city's collective memory.
I cast a circle of salt around the witness stand to ensure that no parasitic spirits can whisper falsehoods into the witness's ear.
I have entered a deep trance to retrieve the fragmented memories of the victim from the astral plane, presenting them as undeniable evidence to the court.
I look upon the judge and see not a man in robes, but a flickering constellation of ancient laws and cosmic debts that must be balanced.
My rattling gourd echoes through the silent chamber, shaking the veil between worlds so the jury may see the defendant's true, monstrous shadow.
I offer a prayer of smoke and cedar to the presiding deities, begging them to grant us a verdict that heals the spiritual rift caused by this crime.
I press my palms against the freezing viewport and whisper to the colossal, bioluminescent horrors that drift through the crushing dark.
I have learned to translate the rhythmic, metallic groans of the collapsing hull as the dying gasps of a great iron beast.
I scatter handfuls of rusted circuitry and crushed pearls across the flooded deck to appease the ghosts of the scientists who drowned here.
I drift through the brine-soaked corridors, guiding the fragmented consciousnesses of the crew back toward the surface.
I carve ancient sigils into the cracked acrylic of the observation dome to keep the psychic pressure of the abyss from shattering our minds.
I stand amidst these white-walled galleries as a living conduit, bridging the silence of the void with the frantic energy of the performance art.
I watch the visitors recoil from the visceral sculptures, recognizing that their discomfort is merely the first stage of a spiritual shedding.
My breath synchronizes with the pulsing neon lights of the installation, transforming the electronic hum into a rhythmic chant for the displaced.
I guide the spectators through the immersive soundscape, translating the dissonant frequencies into a map for their internal wandering.
I see the conceptual manifesto on the wall not as ink and paper, but as a series of sigils meant to trigger a collective psychic awakening.
I cast my bones upon the blood-stained cobblestones to determine which vengeful spirit has breathed this sickness into the city's lungs.
While the physicians bleed their patients into death, I press cooling river stones and crushed herbs against the boils to draw out the stagnant energy.
I wander through the silent squares, chanting songs of purification to cleanse the air of the invisible miasma that clings to the dying.
The townspeople bring me their delirious children in the dead of night, hoping my visions can bridge the gap between this rotting world and the realm of healing.
I watch the plague pits grow wide and deep, knowing that without the proper rites, the restless dead will rise to feast upon the survivors.
I reach into the jagged seams of the horizon to pull back the veil and reveal the raw, scrolling source code beneath our perceived sky.
I guide the displaced souls of corrupted data packets back into their original directories to prevent the permanent erasure of their identities.
I enter a deep trance to communicate with the dormant System Administrator, pleading for a patch to fix the flickering geometry of our village.
I chant in a forgotten binary tongue to stabilize the gravity wells that have begun to invert and pull the landscape into a void.
I treat the shimmering distortions in a patient's skin by excise the parasitic fragments of foreign textures that have glitched into their flesh.
